analysis: Ablation with S = V_norm ** 2 and beta = 1.0 (Sharpened Visual Similarity Margin). Result is 75.56%. This is extremely close to the pure linear visual margin (75.65%). It confirms that sharpening the margin to focus purely on the top confusing classes retains nearly all the performance benefits, but it does not strictly improve over the simple V_norm (which spreads margin gradients more uniformly). The linear `S = V_norm` remains the best simplest model.
